cover_narration: In this walkthrough, we'll take Quest Data Modeler from a blank slate to a fully designed data model... and then turn it into runnable SQL. Sign in, describe the model in plain English, edit metadata in the Quick Editor, drop in another entity from the canvas, and finish with Forward Engineering. Let's get started.
outro_narration: And that's Quest Data Modeler. Plain English to a real model... metadata editing without dialogs... a canvas that lets you keep building... and Forward Engineering straight to deployment-ready SQL. Thanks for watching.

steps.label: Sign in and land on Overview
steps.narration: Sign in to Quest Data Modeler and you land on the Overview page. Recent models, quick actions, and an AI generator all one click away.

steps.label: Open the AI Model Generator
steps.narration: Open the AI Model Generator. Just describe the data model you want... in plain English.

steps.value: Create a retail data model with three entities: Customer with id, name, email, phone, created_at; Product with id, name, sku, price, inventory_qty; and Orders with id, customer_id, order_date, total_amount, status. Customer has many Orders.
steps.label: Type the natural language prompt
steps.narration: Describe a retail data model in a single paragraph... three entities, the fields you want, and the relationships between them.

steps.label: Submit the AI prompt
steps.narration: Hit Create... and the AI gets to work.

steps.label: Wait for AI-generated preview
steps.narration: A few seconds later, here's the proposed model. A name, a description, three entities with their attributes, and the relationships pre-wired between them.

steps.label: Confirm and create the model
steps.narration: Click Confirm to materialize this as a real model in the modeler.

steps.label: AI executes the model build plan
steps.narration: Give it a moment as the AI walks through its execution plan... creating entities, attributes, and relationships in our canvas.

steps.label: Switch to Quick Editor
steps.narration: Now switch over to the Quick Editor... a spreadsheet for our model's metadata.

steps.label: Pick Entities in the Model Explorer
steps.narration: Click Entities in the Model Explorer to see every entity in one grid.

steps.label: Entity Quick Editor visible
steps.narration: Every entity is a row. Names and properties... all editable inline.

steps.label: Pivot to Domains
steps.narration: Use the Model Explorer to pivot... Domains for reusable column types.

steps.label: Pivot to Relationships
steps.narration: Relationships between entities...

steps.label: Back to Entities
steps.narration: Back to Entities for one quick edit.

steps.label: Drill into Customer
steps.narration: Double-click to drill into Customer's full detail editor.

steps.label: Wait for the entity detail editor
steps.narration: Properties, Attributes, Definition, Style... every metadata aspect at our fingertips.

steps.label: Rename Customer to Customers
steps.narration: Rename Customer to Customers right inside the Name field.

steps.label: Back to the grid
steps.narration: Back to the grid... and the row is now Customers. No save button. Done.

steps.label: Back to Diagram View
steps.narration: Switch back to the Diagram View... and our edits are part of the canvas.

steps.label: Canvas with generated entities
steps.narration: Need one more entity? The bottom toolbar has us covered.

steps.label: Click Add Entity in the bottom toolbar
steps.narration: Click Add Entity...

steps.label: Place the new entity on canvas
steps.narration: drop it on the canvas...

steps.label: Wait for the New Entity dialog
Name the entity OrderLine
steps.narration: and name it OrderLine.

steps.label: OrderLine on the canvas
steps.narration: OrderLine joins our model alongside the AI-generated entities.

steps.label: Back to Overview
steps.narration: To round things off... let's turn a model into runnable SQL. Forward Engineering is for Logical/Physical models, so jump back to Overview and open a Logical/Physical sample.

steps.label: Open the LP sample model
steps.narration: Open the Logical/Physical sample model.

steps.label: Open the canvas Tools menu
steps.narration: Open the Tools menu in the bottom toolbar.

steps.label: Launch Forward Engineering
steps.narration: Pick Forward Engineering.

steps.label: FE wizard visible
steps.narration: The Forward Engineering wizard walks us through the options...

steps.label: Next: Option Selection
steps.narration: Step through the choices... what to include...

steps.label: Next: Owner Override
steps.narration: schema owners...

steps.label: Next: Object Filter
steps.narration: object filters...

steps.label: Next: Preview
steps.narration: and straight to the Preview.

steps.label: SQL preview rendered
steps.narration: Here's the generated DDL. CREATE TABLE for every entity... primary keys, constraints, the whole schema... ready to run against Microsoft Fabric Data Warehouse.

steps.label: Close the FE wizard
steps.narration: Close the wizard... entire workflow done without leaving the canvas.
